what_it_does: Acceptance test for Suite.act click-tier governance: it verifies that click tier is resolved by address tier, not by verb. Bare, untiered, and unknown addresses cause a run to execute immediately, while a CONFIRM/LOCKED-tier address causes the same verb to surface as a proposal awaiting operator approval without executing the graph.
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 so surfaced items are tagged with test_origin.
Creates a simple two-node graph (constant -> uppercase) in a temporary FsStore.
Defines a global check(label, cond) helper that increments a PASS counter.
Asserts Suite.act and Suite._tier_for_address exist as the gated seams.
Tests bare run, untiered registry address, and unknown address all route AUTO and execute.
Injects a tiered UI_REGISTRY row with extras.tier='source_data' to obtain a CONFIRM/LOCKED address.
Verifies the CONFIRM-tier run produces did=='surfaced_for_approval', no ran set, a new surfaced item, and reply text containing 'needs approval' and 'not yet acted'.
Tests a normally-AUTO 'show' verb on the same tiered address to prove address-keyed gating.
References external files (App.tsx, governance.py, addresses.json) only in comments.
standouts: The central claim is address-keyed tiering, not verb-keyed tiering.
It frames the bare-run case as a U1 do-not-regress invariant requiring real graph execution.
Discriminating assertions demand actual state movement (ran/cached) rather than just posture.
Tiered addresses are injected into the runtime UI_REGISTRY, never by editing the design-system addresses.json.
conventions: Top-level script with imports, setup, sequential checks, and try/finally cleanup.
Inline comments are dense and serve as the spec narrative.
Uses a lightweight global PASS counter instead of a standard test framework.
concerns: Directly mutates suite.UI_REGISTRY via list reassignment rather than a fixture API.
Relies on internal underscore method _tier_for_address for assertions.
Hardcodes UI reply strings ('needs approval', 'not yet acted') which may be brittle.
References external line numbers and files in comments without verifying them in this file.
Unknown-address behavior depends on the registry fallback implementation not shown here.
notes: An acceptance test that guards the address-keyed click-tier seam by proving bare runs execute while governed addresses surface for approval.
questions: What is the exact schema and field order of a UI_REGISTRY row?
How does _tier_for_address map the string 'source_data' to a CONFIRM/LOCKED class in governance.POLICY?
What is the full contract of Suite.act return values for ran, cached, routed_posture, and surfaced?
How does COMPANY_TEST_RUN propagate into governance.py/inbox tagging?
What do the referenced App.tsx:1362/1169 lines implement, and are they still current?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to acceptance-test address-keyed tier gating, and the file does exactly that through immediate-run cases and a CONFIRM-tier proposal case.
apparent_themes: Regression guard for immediate execution behavior
Separation of runtime registry from design-system address definitions
intention_signals: It is trying to enforce that tier routing is determined by address governance metadata, preserving immediate execution for ungoverned clicks and requiring explicit approval for governed/locked addresses, independent of the verb being clicked.
novelty: The test is framed as a regression guard with 'discriminating assertions' that require real state movement, and it injects tiered addresses into the runtime UI_REGISTRY rather than editing the design-system addresses.json file.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test for the address-keyed tier routing seam, capturing the U1 immediate-execution invariant and the CONFIRM/LOCKED proposal behavior, plus a lightweight check harness and a fixture pattern for injecting tiered UI addresses.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for company Suite.act address-keyed click tiering, verifying immediate execution for bare, untiered, and unknown run clicks and proposal surfacing for CONFIRM/LOCKED-tier addresses regardless of verb.
intention_for_embedding: To prevent regression of immediate-run behavior while ensuring governed addresses require operator see-and-approval before acting.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that checks a condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: A lightweight inline assertion function used throughout the script: it asserts the supplied condition with a labeled failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an 'ok' line. It replaces a standard test framework for this standalone acceptance script.
